videoTitle: Ranking Every Mobile Game Ever 18: Hatsune Miku: Colorful Stage/ Project Sekai
textClean: project sekai/colorful stage has pretty much anything and everything in it. for the rhythm game part: (the number is the difficulty level range) easy (5-10) and normal (10-16) charts for those who have never touched a rhythm game before, you get used to tapping and holding in a steady rhythm until you can make your way up to hard then there's hard (15-25) and expert (21-32) charts for people who are trying to improve at the game, these require more coordination and overall speed and finally master (25-37) charts and these tend to be more pattern focused rather than speed (except the higher level ones which are both) and those tend to be so fun if you can handle them because they have so much freedom to do whatever they want with no difficulty block, look up "seibai itaAAAAAsu master 31" if you want an example, love that chart. and.. i just said master had no difficulty blocks, but what if you managed to break it anyway? you get append (24-38), which is a difficulty you play a different way, with multiple fingers. for some reason most people absolutely despise them because they play with thumbs but i think that because of the ACTUAL no difficulty block, they can have the most fun charts in the game. so now we have the rhythm game part, but what songs exactly do we play? (mostly) japanese vocaloid song covers and original commissioned songs. songs are commissioned for the in-game stories. as mentioned in the video, there are stories in the game, and yes they can be slow paced but honestly, they're extremely well written for a game that's supposed to be a cash grab. they are actually life changing, especially paired with the song born out of them. kashika, donketsu, engeki have all changed my life, you can look them up if you want. some songs have 2DMVs with some nice art and editing, some songs have 3DMVs with some nice choreography, some songs have both and some none and i think that's just another thing in the game, if you draw, video edit or like choreography, those can be good references and you can learn from them. back to the stories, some scenes are illustrated, which are turned into cards that you can pull in the gacha system of the game, and you can use them on your 5 card team to play the rhythm game. they all have skills that increase score but some trades some of that off for healing or perfect locking (turning greats or lower notes to perfect for a few second) and these are pretty nice for people who like to play by their rules. if it feels too hard, these cards can help you. and also there are cards who increase even more score but only on perfect notes. if you don't like any of that you can play rank match when you reach rank 50 and have 10 full combos on expert/master songs. these are absolutely 100% skill based since cards don't do anything and the winner of the 1v1 is based on who scores more points (perfect = 3, great = 2, good = 1, bad and miss = 0), so the person with the highest accuracy wins. the songs are chosen randomly between the current 607 different songs pool and harder songs get chosen the higher your rank is. that's mostly it. there's also virtual live shows and the "mysekai" gamemode which is like a open world social simulation game. the global (english) version which is one year late on updates is in any app store (which is okay, you can play on it if you want) and the japanese version which you can download with a vpn or through an apk or by just being japanese, lol. this was nice to type, and my final thoughts are: if you're all in for the rhythm game, there's probably better ones that you can get addicted to for a while and then quit. but as a more complete game in general, project sekai is definitely the best mobile game i've ever seen. it's really beginner friendly but also has room for more veteran players. even though it's a gacha game and paying does get you an advantage in the event leaderboard, you can absolutely do well anyways since it values effort and consistent playing over than anything. it's what motivated me to learn japanese, too. it really all feels different to understand the stories and songs like how they're originally written, since there are things impossible to translate. project sekai is a great piece of japanese media for practicing japanese considering all of this can be in japanese if you download the japanese version. thanks for reading, mysterious person.

videoTitle: comparison of Bandori and PJSK cover songs (pt. 2)
textClean: This is super long and I might be super late to this, so bear with me. Children Record (reboot) - Afterglow. The key Ran sang it in should have been the key Len sang it in; either that or replace him with Miku. Ego Rock - Neither. This song is in a list of Vocaloid songs that I completely detest. I hate that song. But, if I did have a choice, I'd choose Hello Happy World Hibana -Reloaded- - I like both, but the arrangement of Roselia's version is PEAK! Villain - Afterglow. I think Ran's strong vocals fit the song better. But, I do like Hinata Sato's cover and Mizuki's alt vocal of the song (these are two totally different things, trust me). Melancholic - Poppin'Party. Their version is different and as someone who is a fan of the original with Kagamine Rin V2 and misses the autotune, I think Kasumi's vocals are a breath of fresh air. Also, I like the original version with Rin V2 better than both of them, imo. I'm an old-school Vocaloid fan. Fight me. Kamippoina - Raise A Suilen. I was never a fan of that song either and is in the same list as Ego Rock, but, what settled it for me was LAYER'S vocals. I knew Raychell had a wide vocal range, but I didn’t think she could sing that high Telecaster B-boy - Wonderlands × Showtime. I think this might be my only ProSeka choice because of how high the Afterglow version is and how singable Emu and Nene's version is and I think the key they sing in should have been the key Len sang in in the original. Kyoufuu All-Back Both again, but I like Kokoro's vocals better than Rin (and that is coming from someone who sees her as her favorite Vocaloid). Plus I like the original with Kaai Yuki better, anyway. Dreamin' ChuChu - Pastel*Palettes and Kanon. I like the arrangement and the fact that Kanon gets to sing a bit in this one away from HHW. Otome Dissection - Roselia. Yukina's powerful vocals triumph again. Plus, the song sound better in their style. Non-Breath Oblige - MyGO!!!!! I have heard the actual original song (without Nightcord) with Miku, and I found it impressive that Tomori/Hina Youmiya could sing such a difficult song not exactly meant for humans. Also, the arrangement is PEAK! Kyu-kurarin - Hello Happy World. I think their arrangement is cuter and more expressive and I'm not really a fan of MEIKO and Haruka's vocals in that one. Bocca della Veritá - Raise A Suilen. The production and arrangement is just better imo. Especially in the "ah-ah-ah-ah" parts. Yoidore Shirazu - Roselia. Primarily because I think their version is more bouncy and danceable and it's a different song than what we're used to hearing from Roselia. Bad Apple - Roselia (again). I like the arrangement and the vocals. I so wish they put out a full version, tho. Their version captures the angst more than Nightcord's version, imo. Tokyo Teddy Bear - Roselia (again). There were some vocal choices made in the Leo/Need version that I'm not a fan of, so I automatically give it to Roselia. QUEEN - Morfonica. I like the fact that their version is in a lower key, which is what I wish Sega and Colorful Palette should have considered with MEIKO, instead of having her sing it in the original key. I don't like how she sounds in higher pitched songs. You can disagree with me, if you'd like.

videoTitle: A cynical review of the Hatsune Miku gacha game -  Project Sekai: Colorful Stage
textClean: oh i totally get how you feel... before project sekai's release, i was really bitter. i thought it would never top diva for me, i thought the characters were boring, and i thought the rhythm game was lame. but everything grew on me SO hard. diva was special to me, and as much as i hate how sega tries to profit off of it by making rehashed releases, it will never stop having a special place in my heart. and when i heard of project sekai's release, i was angry because i thought "why would they make original characters when they can make a game with vocaloids alone instead!" or "vocaloids in a story setting again? ugh!" or "this is corporate trash!" and no, the mvs aren't better. the vocaloids don't shine in the story. and the vocaloids aren't even the main focus of the game. but i somehow love the game now. and i think it's because i realized i wanted the game to be something it was never intended to be. the characters are all so fun and unique. i love the conflicts and themes in the story. i love a lot of the charts in the game. so much of the card art and the concepts behind it are incredibly creative. the song selection combines old and new vocaloid songs. and the vocaloid's personalities vary throughout the groups, channeling a part of what vocaloid always is and always should be; varied and artistic. everything in this game is varied, actually. the vocaloid's personalities, the styles, the aethetics, the art, the symbolism; everything. this game never was what i wanted at first; a game that uses vocaloid characters and is just a plain old song list. instead, i got something better, being a game centered around the perspective looking INTO vocaloid. it's the perspective of vocaloid nerds and lovers, or just those who experience the genre in general. this game feels less like a game about vocaloid characters, and more like a love letter to vocaloid as a whole that is contained within a game. it's detailing what vocaloid is to people and how it can help them (side note, one of the themes in 25ji story being about "saving people with songs" made me feel deeply understood. it's something that not only i have noticed in the vocaloid space, but something that i am able to relate to. and seeing that the creators of the game's story understand that..? god. it fills me with a feeling i can't describe. it's like the community i love is finally being understood by artists who can put their work out there and reach a larger audience through a game like this. it just feels... good). vocaloid, something near and dear to me, has been put in hands that i trust. and it's being used so incredibly well. oh also adding this! i just wanted to give my take on leveling cards for your score. i honestly don't think it's that bad. in fact, i think it just adds more to the game. leveling cards just gives me more incentive to play songs. i've found that i don't return to games like project diva (when it's not watching mvs) much anymore because i really have no incentive to play since i've unlocked everything. but the gacha system makes it impossible to own everything, and logically, you can never run out of cards to level because of 2 stars and new 3 stars getting added every week or so. without leveling cards, a lot of fun grinding and other features dependent on card strength would be lost. i don't see it as them saying card stats > skill, but more as it being card stats AND skill matter. with skill, you can get full combo crystals, and with cards, you get score crystals. plus, that makes it so players who struggle with rhythm games as a whole don't miss out on major rewards while still rewarding players for their skill in other ways. but this might also just be because leveling up cards is just satisfying for me alskjf

videoTitle: project sekai is ok I guess 🤷‍♀️
textClean: hello!! i am a big proseka player as a former big bandori player. i totally understand your criticisms and i can see where youre coming from. there are some things that seperate bandori and proseka for me, which is mainly why i moved. warning: this is going to be a semi rant, and a little confusing. but as someone who has played bandori for about 2.5 yrs and proseka for about a year i thought this is my time to shine. 1: proseka is way better at the rhythm game aspect. i know you already covered this, but i also wanted to touch on it. bandoris hardest song is lvl. 30, and albeit hard, it gets boring sometimes when you want to play a hard song and you’re stuck with either that lvl 30 (hell! or hell?) or the two level 29s, which is roku chounen and this other poppin’party song which imo is not charted well. BUT, proseka has a more wide selection. it has more interesting note patterns and better charting. im going to leave this section here because you touched a lot of proseka charting and ill mostly sound like a broken record. 2: master vs. special proseka has something called master mode, and bandori has something called special mode. the difference here is that special mode is just a new chart for the map, it can be harder or easier than expert. but master mode is always a couple levels up to expert which i really enjoy. for example, on proseka, if theres a song that i like but is kind of a bore to play on expert, like its lvl 23 or 24, i can easily switch to master and its a lvl 26 or 27. this also is helped with prosekas good charting because usually in bandori (and as of late especially) special mode chartings arent really that good. the one thing i will say though is that special mode is always available to the player, whilst master mode you have to unlock by playing expert and then getting less than 7 goods, bads and misses all together. you can kinda take that how you will. 3: gacha now im personally a fan of idol/gacha rhythm games, hence why i like proseka and bandori (amongst some others like d4dj for example). i will say that although both gachas are pretty unforgiving, prosekas gacha is way better. first, they have a pity system. 5 ten pulls and you get a guaranteed 4 star. 10 ten pulls and you get a guaranteed featured 4 star from that gacha. second, they have gacha tickets which can be used around the game (permanent/limited gacha tickets). this means if you got 15 spare gacha tickets from one gacha and didnt use it to spark for a specific 4 star, they roll over and you can use those gacha tickets for another gacha (given that its got the same permanent v limited ticket). bandori pretty much has none of this. sure, its got the spark aspect and the gacha ticket (in a different way, kinda groups all of it together), it doesnt have the pity or the perm. lim which i think is better. 4: other aspects (merch/anime/etc.) bandori wins in this aspect. obviously this is unfair, because they have been running for way longer than proseka (bandori is running its 5.5 anniversary on jp, and proseka is running its 2 yrs anniversary on jp as well), there is still a lot of bandori merch that had been pumped out. like, so much. posters, manga, 3 season anime, countless figures, nendoroids, plushes, keyrings, mini series, and a LOT of clothing items, but etc. but, i will say that for only 2 year anniversary proseka has also pumped out a lot of merch. this includes stuff like even more plushes, some figures, 2 nendoroids, keyrings, acrylic stand ups?? or whatever theyre called, and honestly a lot more stuff that is mostly avaliable in japan. even as a merch collector i dont think i know all of the proseka merch that is out there (despite my scouring). so, after all this, i just thought i would give a slightly indepth comment rant about proseka vs bandori. did you only mention bandori for like 10 seconds? yeah. but, im mostly hoping that this might give you a little more insight between them.

My original take was that kohane doesnt fit as leader but i saw wayyy too many ppl with that as their hot take so im gonna change it: I like the idea of commissioning songs from vocaloid producers but hate the execution oml while all the comms are bangers and i have a whole playlist with all of them, it doesnt make much sense ingame as in it creates sm inconsistency like mmj is probably the most consistent all their songs feel like real idol songs and fit them really well mmj themselves have a more cute and lively idol concept but even with songs like supernova and antiyou they still feel like they fit with the idol vibe overall mmj comms feel like the producers really understood the assignment this is not the case for vbs who is probably the least consistent with their music. Vbs is the street group and as someone who grew up on street and vocaloid when i first saw them i was super excited just to get hit with forward (i do love forward) but forward...for the street group??? Vbs is clearly a western inspired group they even have an arc where they go to america so where is that in the music??? Theyre more of a pop groups that wear street styled clothing rather than actually being street. We have metal from fuel, jazz from blender, pop from beat eater, ultra c sounds more like something youd hear at a club than on the street, obvs these are all good songs but street where??? Even their more "street" songs are just pop songs with a short rap verse (and the raps are only in a few songs) it bothers me that they dont have a true signature sound they switch genres every other song and i feel its mostly due to pjsk not having their own team making these songs. I think it mostly bothers me bc as someone who plays other rhythm games, these games dont struggle as much with song consistency bc most of them have teams dedicated to making their music while pjsk relys on outside forces (the vocaloid producers) and obviously not every producer makes the same or even similar music, units like mmj or leoneed dont struggle with this alot bc they mostly get commisions from producers that generally make music within those genres, wxs and n25 are mostly consistent but vbs is the worst offender💔💔💔 i probably didnt word half of this right but oh well lmao

videoTitle: bandori covers after project sekai feels like
textClean: Pjsk has Niigo/N25 No all jokes aside, imo the characters are darker and the songs are generally songs that people in the Vocaloid fandom would actually know, versus Bandori songs, I seriously don’t know half of them and it kinda makes me not care about them. Also the gameplay in Bandori is… yeah, a bit weird. Project Sekai is actually so much smoother than what I expected. Also, I personally just don’t like Bandori’s art style as much, it feels more stiff and “2000’s anime” than Pjsk does. And, long rant here! I know I already talked about the stories being better, but I honestly, really do think Niigo/N25 and maybe also VBS is Pjsk’s selling point. When I first downloaded Pjsk, I genuinely didn’t think I’d play it for more than a month, but here I am, 7 months later… People who have played rhythm games can easily see tropes like childhood friends (L/N), cute idols (MMJ), and “:D WE ARE HAPPY!!!” (WxS), and I’m not saying they’re bad (I absolutely ADORE MMJ so much!) but it’s just… we’ve seen those already, y’know? They don’t really bring anything new to the table. With VBS, you get street singers and angst, with Niigo/N25, … It’s just full of angst and pain, honestly, and the characters are so relatable that it hurts. Kanade’s savior complex and inability for self care, Mafuyu’s lost sense of self and her extremely relatable (especially to Asians) home situation, Ena’s frustrations with her hobby and the fact she’s very obviously not the best person but just a normal person, and Mizuki being an outcast in school who pretends everything is fine and acts as the mediator… Honestly everyone has got situations that people would relate to. It’s genuinely refreshing how these things are actually getting shown in media, and for me the dark themes make them so much more enjoyable because I don’t feel like I’m just watching yet another group of happy fictional people whose only issues are “oh no I accidentally spilled my drink uwu…” because, that’s not realistic, for one, and that makes me feel very disconnected. I love happy characters, don’t get me wrong, but when they’re always seen as only happy, I like them a lot less. (Probably part of why I actually like WxS and MMJ despite them all being happy go lucky.) With that said, :D people have different tastes so I can’t speak for everyone! Some people simply like Bandori because they like certain characters, or they like the music, or whatever else. That’s completely fine, I’m just saying why I prefer Pjsk over Bandori.

Hot Take: Who the hell writes for VBS? Not saying it’s bad, Akito is my favorite character, VBS is my favorite unit. But they seem shallow, bland. Everything is about their careers and passion, and I am tired. We get more of WXS’s characters as a people, even if they are going overseas or whatever. We get to see the idols having personal struggles. But VBS? They had their best moments between a Kashika event and the Gekokujou event. But now that they surpassed Rad Weekend? It would be the perfect opportunity to write about Burnout, or how it’s okay for yours dreams to not make sense anymore, or to take a break, anything. But the “Let’s conquer the world” doesn’t seem…. believable for me. Also hate how they don’t bring their parents on this. Or make the “father figure mentor” a thing. Acknowledge that maybe Akito doesn’t have a proper support on his dream, that’s why he rather resort to Ken or people on the vivid street. Make Kohane have a backstory. Maybe her parents worry too little, that’s why she got along with Taiga. Idk GIVE ME ANYTHING Touya used to be a character that could hold events on his own, now? He basically needs Tsukasa or Akito to function on an event, he supports those characters, he’s funny with them. He doesn’t work “alone” anymore. Make him interesting again! Maybe he wants to have a solo career! Who knows? Not saying that he should leave VBS, but it would be interesting to see him publishing his own songs and still working with VBS, that normal to happen! All that yapping, I just think VBS seems like it’s out of things to do. They achieved their dream, now what? I have a feeling that PJSK it’s running out of things to do. Wouldn’t surprise me if they “ended” those characters stories on the 6th anniversary or something. I just think VBS it’s being “left out” the most by the writers.

I hope rui5/kasa6 are really really good, because honestly, kasa4/5 and rui4 were really dissapointing. Nene5 was a bit better, but the stories just dont have what the older events had. I still love the games story, but the parts I really love are all over a year old now Im honestly kind of disappointed with the direction they took the story. They started out pretty good, but the newer ones all feel disappointing ESPECIALLY wxs, the disband arc was so gripping, and I loved it, and then after our happy ending, the new arc is basically just fluff... Ive seen so many people who used to play prsk quit/lose interest, I really really love the game, but i want them to do better, it feels like the latest third of prsk was at least 80% fluff I do like mizu5, but i think if they hadnt done it, prsk stories mightve been better as a whole, i dont like angst that characters get over in 10 minutes, I want something more realistic, looming, bittersweet, like wxs disband arc, it was so so good, but then they got way too comfortable, the characters are good in theiry, but the execution has been especially lacking for over a year now

Personally I never really felt attached to any of the characters of L/N. They all feel so bland compared to the other units. Most of the units have smth unique like comedy-WXS or social status’s-Niigo (most of the characters deal with problems based on others) but L/N really only focuses on their friendships and ngl it was kind of hard to get through. I’ve also seen some of the side stories and they all kind of feel repetitive with Shiho only wanting to practice most of the time and there’s not much development compared to the end of the main story. Like Mizuena become closer since the main story and Mafuyu is starting to learn that her mom’s controlling her (and breaking away) but L/N just feels like the same thing over and over again, at least to me. I also feel like a lot of the leaders are also not the greatest choice. Like u said, Tsukasa doesnt rlly feel like he’s the leader most of the time (I haven’t finished the main story tho) and Ichika was really left out in the main story. She really didn’t do much (but I also haven’t watched it in a while so I might be wrong) Smth unrelated: I hate the distribution between L/N and WXS. Ichika is ALWAYS in ALL of the songs. I love her voice but sometimes I’d rather see other characters be in the main lead bc when u see who sings different songs. You really don’t have much of a variety of the characters. Usually it’s Ichika and Shiho (singing at diff pitches which I don’t enjoy) or Ichika and Honami. I don’t think I’ve really heard a Ichika x Saki duo song even tho their the closest out of everyone. And in WXS, EMU. Oh my goodness. U can HARDLY hear anyone else in the main chorus. She has a really loud voice which isn’t my fav so I might be biased but it’s so annoying. Like Tsukasa and Rui have nice voices but I hand ever hear them bc of Emu. I love her dearly but she’s ALWAYS singing so LOUDDDD. And the duo covers for WXS and MMJ r also always the same. Tsukasa X Emu, Rui X Nene, Minori X Haruka, and Airi X Shizuku. Always the same. And it’s worse bc my fav pair is Haruka X Shizuku bc I love their vocals especially in Shoujo Rei but we don’t get much of them in much of their commissioned or event songs (idk I don’t know which songs r which in MMJ) I’d also like to see some An X Akito, I’d feel fresh not having the same old pairs. And for Niigo and WXS having a single character running their Sekai’s, it’s unique to the group but aside from the main stories, u don’t hear much abt it again. Like Mafuyu literally SENT THE GIRLS HOME and after that they don’t speak abt it again or anything. Until recently, there’s been a LOT of Mizuki events especially with the intersecting ones so I’m glad that we’re getting more development on Mafuyu but what abt Kanade? I’m like halfway through the events but the only REAL one I remember is when Mizuki took them for a tour so she could get inspiration. We also haven’t gotten many updates abt her dad. It would also be cool if they would NOT include the virtual singers every now and then. Like I LOVE Charles and villain bc of it. I feel like Niigo has better tuning with the VS’s but L/N is really done dirty. I’d be nice to have a song dedicated to JUST the characters. Sorry if there’s any mistakes it’s like 1 in the morning rn so I’m kinda making this in a rush. Anyway those r my opinions. I also think it’s be cool for a gender end event.

videoTitle: BanG Dream! Girls Band Party!☆PICO Episode 24 (with English subtitles)
textClean: Also in BanG Dream! and Love Live! featuring South Park and the Eurovision Song Contest: Bigger, Longer and Uncut, there will be what's call "the Eurovision Song Contest Marathon Challenge (Live Performance Version)", basically think the Endless Setlist from Rock Band, except you play through all the songs of a edition of the Eurovision Song Contest, ordered by country in alphabetical order for the 1956 edition, by the placement of the song from last place to first place for the 1957 to the 1968 edition, the same goes for the 1969 edition, but because of 4 countries having the same amount of points, which leads to a tie (this led to a tie-breaker rule being implmented), the order is like this: United Kingdom fourth-to-last because they already host the contest in 1968, Spain third-to-last because they hosted the contest in 1969, France is second-to-last and Netherlands the last song of the 1969 edition of the Eurovision Song Contest Marathon Challenge because they hosted the contest in 1970, also by the placement of the song from last place to first place for the 1970 to 2003 edition and then by the amounts of points that the songs achieved for both the Semi-Final songs and the Grand Final songs from 2004 onwards. The amount of songs depends on the editions but the editions before the Semi-Final system was implmented always never exceeds 26 songs, after the Semi-Final system is implmented, the number of participating country and, as a result the number of songs has exceeds the number 30 and some editions has exceeds the 40 mark, the edition with the most songs are: 2008 and 2011, both editions has 43 songs, which means, even though those song are 3 minutes in length, it's still a Marathon Level and this also applies to any editions after 2004. But beating it will grant you a big amount of currency, both of which comes from BanG Dream! Girls Band Party and Love Live! School Idol Festival, for every song you get through without draining your hit point bar you get 1 million Gs (the currency from Love Live! School Idol Festival) and 100 thousand Stars (the currency from BanG Dream! Girls Band Party) and the number multiply by two if you get the Full Combo Notes and multiply by four if you get the All Perfect Notes and doing both multiply the number by eight, if, for example: you do the 2008 edition of the Eurovision Song Contest Marathon Challenge you will get 43 millions Gs and 430 thousand Stars, if you Full Combo a certain amount of song, like say 20 out of 43 songs, you will get 83 million Gs and 830 thousand Stars, if you both do a Full Combo Notes and All Perfect Notes, you will get 203 million Gs and 2 milion and 30 thousand Stars, if you Full Combo Notes and All Perfect Notes on all the songs, you will get 344 milions Gs and 3 milions and 440 thousand Stars, also, if you die somewhere, how much Gs and Stars you will get depends on how far you get through, how many of the song you have Full Combo Notes and All Perfect Note. Basically it's as i said the Endless Setlist from Rock Band with the massive reward for doing 50 Laps of the Goliath race in any Forza Horizon fron the 2nd game onwards. And then there's "the Eurovision Song Contest Marathon Challenge (Studio Version)" and another one called "the Eurovision Song Contest Marathon Challenge (Uncut Versrion)", the former is exactly the same things as the "(Live Performance Version)", except it used the Eurovision recording of the song and the latter is essentally the same thing as the "(Live Performance Version)", except it use the original recording of the song, which isn't cut, shorten or censored.

videoTitle: The Wild World of Project Sekai Unpopular Opinions
textClean: i often see posts that are like “niigos stories all revolve around mafuyu, and it makes me angry.” first of all, its normal and okay to support your friends when they are struggling, especially with someone like mafuyu who literally has a fake persona that she puts on around others. i feel like the reason some of kanade’s focuses are connected to mafuyu is because mafuyu matters to her and the two found each other at a point in time where both of them were despairing. also, kanade’s character and her “goal” is centered around “making music that can save someone”, so i think it’s okay that her focuses are centered around… making music that can… save mafuyu. it’s not everyone’s goal to be the best person, some people want to support others. kanade is a prime example, as a person who always put others before herself. her “character goal” is to reach a point where her needs and others are more balanced, but she inevitably won’t start putting herself before everyone when that time comes, as she’s always been a person that cares about others. as for the other members, mafuyu becomes a metaphor of change being possible. she started as a person who didn’t have any idea of what she wanted in life, and had thought it would’ve been better to disappear. years later, she’s doing way better mentally, has formed genuine connections with people who want the best for her, has realized that her mother might be more controlling than she thought to the point where she doesn’t want to live in the same household, has talked to her mother about her true feelings, and has confided in some who she didn’t know much about before, her father; a person that since childhood hasn’t been present because of work context, yet still wants the best for her and cares for her. niigo has always been centered around personal goals, the digest anime literally being subtitled “SELF”. i interpret niigo’s symbol being based around “even if you have your own goals, having people that support and care about you to push yourself to new heights is important.” this is particularly demonstrated in the recent mafuyu focus event; “reeling in the lights” (mafu5), where mizuki’s words help mafuyu take the step that is confiding in her father, which inevitably pushes her to want to take that same step in confiding in ena as well (let’s hope mizu5 makes that happen). just because the group is usually helping mafuyu, that doesn’t mean that mafuyu’s improvement and presence isn’t doing the same. anyway, niigo is my favorite group and it saddens me whenever i see people just write them off as “the mafuyu group”. i think they are beautifully written, and i hope you can somewhat see my vision after reading this.

What I like about Leo/need being behind is that none of the characters are naturally gifted or quite as skilled (For example, Kohane is talented and Minori is skilled) in their field. Sure, Shiho is probably the most experienced and the best player, but they are also trying to go pro as a band with no connections and are building up from the ground. Sure, they were in a band together when they were younger, but the only one that had continued practicing all this time was Shiho (that I recall) Emu already is the daughter of the owner of PXL, and already had a stage there. She found people that were already talented and that just focused more on teamwork and personal issues An is the daughter of Ken, and Toya and Akito were already in a group themselves and had been preforming. Kohane was the only one who actually needed to improve, but again, she was already a good singer despite not even trying to build her voice up over the years. The main issue with Kohane in the main story is her stage fright. I haven't read any of the key stories for VBS so I don't know where else their story goes, but nonetheless, they go to live houses, An already has a reputation, etc. More More Jump consists of three formerly famous idols, and Minori, who has been working to become an idol her whole life. Of course they'd gain a following much easier. I don't really need to explain much here. They're already previously successful idols Nightcord has already been established for quite some time when we are introduced to them, so they've already gained quite the following. They're an online group shrouded in mystery, and we're not actually sure how many followers they have, but they're not trying to become a professional band, they're just posting music online, which requires no prior connections. It does help that Kanade is insanely talented and that her father was a musician so she had access to music all her life. She has been composing for years. Leo/need feels like it's more based in reality. It's a group of childhood friends becoming a band. Yes, they preform on stages or at live houses, but they don't have connections in their field like An does, being Ken's daughter. She already has a reputation of her own as well, considering she's been preforming for years. Every group has someone that has been in some sort of spotlight for years EXCEPT Leo/need and I think that's where this issue of their slowness stems from. And although Shiho is related to Shizuku, they're in completely separate fields. There's also the fact that Shiho won't even consider letting them preform live until they get good enough to, so there's a lot of staggering in the plotline for that. They are behind, but it's realistic for them to be Also off topic but am I the only one that thinks An should have been the VBS leader? She's the only person of the main stories to have herself as an opening instead of the literal leader. I thought she was the leader for the longest time

While I agree that WxS makes theater productions seem unrealistically easy, they actually have mentioned a lot of things you listed if you actually read the event and side stories. It's said that they usually do 2 hours of warm-ups and training before they actually start practice, and in phoenix event they also participated in flexibility, endurance, and strength training etc (forgot the details but they ran like 10km and did 300 squats lol, nene died). For the costume designs, I don't think it's necessary to be mentioned as you can easily read between the lines-- one of Tsukasa's hobbies is costume-making, and Mizuki also made Rui and Nene's initial costumes. WxS also has a whole ass company backing them, so yeah. As for the scripts, I think it also has been mentioned that Tsukasa is mainly the one who writes them, while Rui does the basic choreography, then all four develop it together. Idk about the extras, but they have utilized the virtual singers in their show once (plus Rui could easily just make robots for that), and it's pretty common in theater for a person to play two or more roles. Lighting and sound is definitely Rui's job tho since he's the director (he's been shown taking care of those technical stuff multiple times). But most of all, they're all overachieving workaholic losers who put A LOT of effort into their shows (especially early on, where they had to put like 5 shows a day to gain an audience). Also, I don't think common sense really applies to WxS since they're literally described as a group of eccentrics lol. Emu has always been crazy athletic from the start, Nene's singing has been acknowledged by a world-famous songstress, Rui is a genius director, and Tsukasa is uhh. He tries /affectionately. BUT in a card story he's able to learn an entire dance choreo perfectly in under 5 minutes, and lots of professionals also praised his acting skills, so he's def talented. Furthermore, they're all characters who already have years of theater experience from the get-go; Emu obviously trying to keep Wonder Stage alive, Nene joining a theater group during middle school and being talented enough to get the lead role, Rui doing unauthorized shows everywhere, and Tsukasa making shows for Saki (plus his part-time jobs). Tho I agree with you on the song-writing part since Tsukasa's so far the only one confirmed to be able to play an instrument(?). But I think that's just a game thing and the songs aren't actually canon bc only a few of them sound musical-like anyway LOL (SORRY IF I SOUND AGGRESSIVE BTW i meant this in a lighthearted way but ended up rambling bc im both a theater AND wxs nerd lmao </3)

I totally understand your point about Shiho, and honestly, I was like that too at first before [somehow] she became my favorite over Saki [who WAS my fav]. I think the biggest issue is the fact that it's a big combination of factors, it was like blind for me because I didn't know a lot about her at first but just settling and understanding her perspective somewhat makes me enjoy her. I acknowledge how Shiho can be perceived as unlikeable because of how she pushes people away and how rude she can be [which I understand people don't like her personality sometimes] and that's a factor you can use to dislike her. But if you ask me, judging her purely off the fact that she wants to go pro and serious about her band is a slight bit unfair. The main thing is that Shiho didn't experience what Saki did, either party can try to understand one other but they never will experience the exact thing. While Saki was in the hospital, Shiho [well not moved on] had a goal, created and had the foundation of an idea of what she wanted to do in life. The friend group fell apart and she can't wait forever for them as unfortunate as that is. When Saki came back, she more so focused on living the life she missed which is totally valid. But expecting your old childhood friend who's got a goal in mind and to spend a good portion of her time with you for something that probably takes her a lot of work is also expected to not work. Shiho is thinking of things Saki is not, and Saki is thinking of things Shiho is not. I don't think one is selfish nor one is childish for choosing one side of a thing or wanting a certain thing. It's just a matter of entertaining both sides views, although Saki's is easier since it's inherently more empathic in nature. EDIT: almost forgot about this point but Of L/N being very stuck on like them being a legit thing and a music group I see it more of a "what advantages does everyone have to get their goal" MMJ is the easiest example, yes being Idols are hard but you literally have Minori who trained her childhood wanting this and 3 whole fledged idols who have experience in the industry WxS has Emu which is really big if you think about it, while yes, that's one person and it doesn't make up for everyone else, the fact that they HAVE a stage to perform on in a popular area already boosts their chances enough N-25 is a bit more different due to how online music works, it's a matter of the internet and that makes it complex, but you've seen how easily songs can blow up especially online in a niche community VBS is not exactly too different from L/N if you ask me, while yes, VBS is more successful they both hold their fair amount of failures over their performance events if you ask me, plus Kohane gets professionally "trained" [although untraditonally], Toya has a background in music already, and An was raised on Vivid street I don't understand what being in a band means but if you ask me, L/N is more akin and realistic to what professional music groups might have to go through from the scrap

insane shiho take honestly. for someone who loves to claim leo/need have no development, you sure seem to have missed every event where shiho developed as a character. also not to pull the ableist card but i feel like it‘s pretty clearly implied that she’s autistic, at least it reads like that when most of her “rudeness,” just comes from an inability to read social cues. also if you bothered to read, you’d know that shiho’s passion for music and her lifelong dream to make a career out of her talent was undermined and devalued when she found people she wanted to share her passion with. and when she finally did find leo/need who were willing to share her dreams with her, she was obviously worried that they would see her as unfriendly and too attached to her instrument like her last band did. exactly like how saki’s willing to overwork herself and get sick because of her abandonment issues. both of them are paranoid, especially in their earlier stories, because of how scared they are to lose the seemingly perfect conditions that their friendship offers them, because at that point they hadn’t realized their roles in the band and really realized how unconditionally the others would support them. shiho first has this realization that leo/need wouldn’t give up on her in resonating with you, but it’s in don’t lose faith that it’s really shown how much trust she has in them, and how leo/need is an integral part of her dream. because if going pro was all she wanted, she could have easily just taken iori up on her offer. she could have easily just accepted the record deal saku sent her that wanted to devalue saki’s (and ichika’s) compositions. she could have easily just let her friends remain at a lower level than her so she could be noticed individually, like she has so many times before. but that’s not what she wants. she loves leo/need, she wants to uplift them, to share her lifelong dream with them. she has the utmost faith in them. also it’s no wonder ever leo/need member is below her musically and that she’s the only one doing as well (as you said) as the other characters in the game. honami dropped her instrument in middle school, ichika is just now learning how to sing, and even though saki’s played the piano for probably longer than shiho’s played the bass, she was in the hospital for years. speaking as a musician myself, even a few weeks off your instrument takes months work of practice to recover. she doesn’t care that they’re at the level they’re at, she’s just worried that they’ll be like her former band mates who fell behind because of their lack of commitment.
